question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Site printer and queue incidents (printer jobs vanishing; network/VLAN issues)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 22:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: No — this simply reports incidents (printer jobs vanishing; network/VLAN issues) and does not propose any action or recommendation.
groundedness_reason: Ticket TI-1622483 documents print jobs disappearing from the queue and print failures, and identifies a newly introduced printer VLAN and missing network/firewall allowance between the print server and printer VLAN as the root cause.

Activities
Emails:
Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22
From: Contact Person 08
To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company
Hello everyone
Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message:
0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed
SMTP server not responding
Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected.
Internet connectivity is generally working.
Could you please check this urgently?
Kind regards
Contact Person 08
14.01.25 08:21
Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox
Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08
Priority: High
14.01.25 08:29
Ticket assigned: Employee 34
Status changes
14.01.25 08:35
Status: In Progress
Notes:
14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34
Contact established with Contact Person 08.
Additional information:
• Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11)
• Webmail (OWA) works
• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending
• Receiving works
Initial assessment:
→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting
Actions performed:
• Recreated Outlook profile
• Removed and re-added account
• Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS)
• Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning
• Checked client firewall
Result: No improvement
14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No
Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay
14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation
Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist).
Reason:
Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy
14.01.25 11:15
Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823
Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering
14.01.25 11:16
Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07
Further analysis (System Engineer)
15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs
21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback
All users can send normally again.
No further issues observed.
Ticket closure
22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK
Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk

groundedness_reason: The ticket describes an Exchange Hybrid incident where emails were stuck in locally installed Outlook (Outbox), SMTP AUTH was found disabled, and the case was escalated to an Exchange specialist—directly matching the answer.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Scan‑to‑Mail / SMTP relay size‑reject diagnostics and remediation
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 36:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail fails for larger documents
Ticket number: TI-1624682
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 28
Owner: Employee 34
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 15.01.26 08:57
Closure
Closure date: 23.01.26 15:36
Closure text: SMTP and mail size limit adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail stable.
Activities
15.01.26 08:45 – Email from Contact Person 28
Good morning
We have noticed that larger scanned documents are not delivered via email.
Small documents (1–2 pages) work normally.
Larger PDFs (approx. 20–30 pages) are not delivered internally or externally.
No clear error message appears on the printer.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
15.01.26 08:57
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 34.
15.01.26 09:30 – Initial analysis
Test scan with one page → delivery successful.
Test scan with 25 pages → no delivery.
Printer displays “Send successful”, but no email in inbox.
15.01.26 10:20 – Mail log review
Internal mail server logs show no incoming message for large files.
SMTP relay log shows entry “Message size exceeds fixed limit”.
16.01.26 09:05 – Inquiry to customer
Were there any recent changes to the mail environment or security policies?
16.01.26 10:10 – Customer response
Two weeks ago, the maximum email size was reduced to limit spam.
We were not aware that this could impact the printers.
17.01.26 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail defines:
• Sending occurs via dedicated SMTP relay
• Maximum file size must be aligned with mail infrastructure
• Changes to mail server policies must be coordinated with scan systems
Finding:
New limit = 10 MB
Scanned PDFs exceed this limit at higher resolution.
20.01.26 – Further analysis
Scan settings on device checked.
Default resolution 600 dpi.
PDF compression disabled.
21.01.26 – Test phase
Temporary reduction of scan resolution to 300 dpi.
Test scan with 25 pages → successful.
Parallel coordination with mail team.
Maximum size moderately increased.
22.01.26 – Configuration adjustment
Scan profile adjusted according to blueprint:
• Default resolution reduced
• PDF compression enabled
• Mail limit adjusted
23.01.26 14:50 – Customer feedback
Several larger documents tested.
Internal and external delivery successful.
23.01.26 15:36 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 28
The root cause was a discrepancy between the scan configuration and the reduced maximum email size in the SMTP relay.
The settings were adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works reliably, including for larger documents.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is a topical title describing diagnostics and remediation for SMTP size-rejects, but it does not itself issue a recommendation or instruct specific actions.
groundedness_reason: The ticket describes Scan-to-Mail failures for large PDFs with the SMTP relay log showing 'Message size exceeds fixed limit', and documents adjustments to scan resolution, PDF compression, and SMTP/mail size limits as remediation, so diagnostics and remediation for SMTP size-reject are directly supported.

Closure date: 22.08.25 16:12
Closure text: Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to Teams Federation Blueprint. Presence display correct again.
Activities
12.08.25 08:32 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Since last week, our external project partners in Microsoft Teams are permanently displayed as “Offline”, although they state that they are online.
Chats are partially delayed, calls are sometimes not signaled.
Internal users see each other correctly.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
12.08.25 08:47
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 37.
12.08.25 09:20 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Does this affect all external organizations or only specific partners?
Were any recent changes made to Teams or security policies?
Thank you.
12.08.25 10:05 – Customer response
It mainly affects partner company “TechBuild AG”.
With other companies, it appears to work.
Two weeks ago, we revised our Teams security policies (made external communication more restrictive).
12.08.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test with TechBuild AG:
• Presence displayed as “Offline”
• Chat delivered
• Status does not change
• External calls sometimes do not ring
Test with another partner company → works normally.
13.08.25 08:55 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the Federation and External Access configuration.
Can you confirm whether the SIP domain of TechBuild AG has recently changed?
Kind regards
13.08.25 10:22 – Customer response
No, the domain remains unchanged.
However, we noticed that TechBuild recently migrated to Direct Routing.
13.08.25 14:10 – Teams Admin Center analysis
External Access Policy reviewed.
Domain techbuild.ch is still allowed.
However, in the new policy, “Presence sharing” was set to “Limited”.
Correlation unclear.
14.08.25 09:30 – Internal log analysis
Teams PowerShell checked:
Get-CsTenantFederationConfiguration
Observation:
AllowPublicUsers = False
AllowFederatedUsers = True
However, “EnablePresencePrivacyMode” is active.
14.08.25 11:45 – Comparison with internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Federation & External Access – Version 1.4”
Section 4 describes:
• Difference between Federation and Presence Sharing
• Impact of Privacy Mode
• Behavior with restrictive policies
• Required settings for partners using Direct Routing
15.08.25 – Internal test
Temporary adjustment of presence settings in test tenant.
Test with external domain.
Presence displayed correctly.
Reverted to production configuration →
External presence again permanently “Offline”.
18.08.25 – Coordination with customer
New security policy introduced to restrict external presence information.
This policy conflicts with Federation standard.
19.08.25 – Adjustment
Configuration adjusted according to Federation Blueprint.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
21.08.25 – Customer feedback
External partners are again correctly displayed as “Available” or “Busy”.
Chats arrive without delay.
Calls are signaled again.
22.08.25 16:12 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was a configuration deviation in the area of Federation and Presence Sharing following a policy adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Teams Federation architecture.
Presence display is now functioning correctly again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: The phrase 'requiring blueprint fixes' implies a needed action/remediation for incidents, which constitutes a forward-looking recommendation.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15

06.11.25 08:37
